sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: εἶἑν · φίλας μὲν ἡδονὰς ἀσπασμάτων
sections.translation: Well ; I have the loving pleasures of your greeting and later I will give them back in turn . You , old man , for you have come at the right time , tell me , what should I do to avenge myself on my father ’ s murderer

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: μητέρα τε κοινωνὸν ἀνοσίων γάμων ;
sections.translation: and on my mother , the partner in his unholy marriage ? Do I still have any well-disposed friends in Argos ? Or am I wholly bankrupt , just as my fortunes are ? With whom shall I ally myself ? By night or day ? What course shall I take against my enemies ?

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , οὐδεὶς δυστυχοῦντί σοι φίλος .
sections.translation: Child , you have no friend in your misfortune . For this thing is a godsend indeed , to share in common , both good and bad . But you — for you have been destroyed from the foundations , in the eyes of your friends , and you have left them no hope — hear it from me and know :

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: ἐν χειρὶ τῇ σῇ πάντʼ ἔχεις καὶ τῇ τύχῃ ,
sections.translation: all that you have is in your own arm and fortune , to win your father ’ s home and your city .

sections.label: Section 612
sections.original: τί δῆτα δρῶντες τοῦδʼ ἂν ἐξικοίμεθα ;
sections.translation: What must I do then to accomplish this ?

sections.label: Section 613
sections.original: κτανὼν Θυέστου παῖδα σήν τε μητέρα .
sections.translation: Kill Thyestes ’ son and your mother .

sections.label: Section 614
sections.original: ἥκω ʼπὶ τόνδε στέφανον · ἀλλὰ πῶς λάβω ;
sections.translation: I have come for that victor ’ s crown ; but how shall I grasp it ?

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: τειχέων μὲν ἐλθὼν ἐντὸς οὐδʼ ἂν εἰ θέλοις .
sections.translation: Not by going within the walls , even if you wanted to .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: φρουραῖς κέκασται δεξιαῖς τε δορυφόρων ;
sections.translation: Is he well furnished with garrisons and body-guards ?

sections.label: Section 617
sections.original: ἔγνως · φοβεῖται γάρ σε κοὐχ εὕδει σαφῶς .
sections.translation: You have understood ; for , clearly , he is afraid of you , and does not sleep .

sections.label: Section 618
sections.original: εἶἑν · σὺ δὴ τοὐνθένδε βούλευσον , γέρον .
sections.translation: Well ; it is for you to plan the next step , old man .

sections.label: Section 619
sections.original: κἀμοῦ γʼ ἄκουσον · ἄρτι γάρ μʼ ἐσῆλθέ τι .
sections.translation: Hear me then ; for something has just come to me .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ἐσθλόν τι μηνύσειας , αἰσθοίμην δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: May you reveal something good , and may I understand it .

sections.label: Section 621
sections.original: Αἴγισθον εἶδον , ἡνίχʼ εἷρπον ἐνθάδε .
sections.translation: I saw Aegisthus , when I was on my way here .

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: προσηκάμην τὸ ῥηθέν . ἐν ποίοις τόποις ;
sections.translation: I approve what you have said . Where was he ?

sections.label: Section 623
sections.original: ἀγρῶν πέλας τῶνδʼ ἱπποφορβίων ἔπι .
sections.translation: Near these fields , at his stables .

sections.label: Section 624
sections.original: τί δρῶνθʼ ; ὁρῶ γὰρ ἐλπίδʼ ἐξ ἀμηχάνων .
sections.translation: What was he doing ? I see some hope , out of difficulties .

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: Νύμφαις ἐπόρσυνʼ ἔροτιν , ὡς ἔδοξέ μοι .
sections.translation: He was preparing a feast for the Nymphs , I thought .

sections.label: Section 626
sections.original: τροφεῖα παίδων ἢ πρὸ μέλλοντος τόκου ;
sections.translation: In return for the bringing up of children or for a coming birth ?

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα πλὴν ἕν · βουσφαγεῖν ὡπλίζετο .
sections.translation: I only know this : he was preparing to sacrifice an ox .

sections.label: Section 628
sections.original: πόσων μετʼ ἀνδρῶν ; ἢ μόνος δμώων μέτα ;
sections.translation: With how many men ? Or alone with his slaves ?

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: οὐδεὶς παρῆν Ἀργεῖος , οἰκεία δὲ χείρ .
sections.translation: No Argive was there , but a band of his own servants .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: οὔ πού τις ὅστις γνωριεῖ μʼ ἰδών , γέρον ;
sections.translation: Surely there isn ’ t anyone who will know me if he sees me , old man ?

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: δμῶες μέν εἰσιν , οἳ σέ γʼ οὐκ εἶδόν ποτε .
sections.translation: There are slaves , who have never even seen you .

sections.label: Section 632
sections.original: ἡμῖν ἂν εἶεν , εἰ κρατοῖμεν , εὐμενεῖς ;
sections.translation: Would they be well disposed to me , if I should prevail ?

sections.label: Section 633
sections.original: δούλων γὰρ ἴδιον τοῦτο , σοὶ δὲ σύμφορον .
sections.translation: Yes , for that is the way of slaves , luckily for you .

sections.label: Section 634
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἂν αὐτῷ πλησιασθείην ποτέ ;
sections.translation: However might I approach him then ?

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: στείχων ὅθεν σε βουθυτῶν ἐσόψεται .
sections.translation: By going where he will see you as he sacrifices .

sections.label: Section 636
sections.original: ὁδὸν παρʼ αὐτήν , ὡς ἔοικʼ , ἀγροὺς ἔχει ;
sections.translation: He has fields by the road , it seems ?

sections.label: Section 637
sections.original: ὅθεν γʼ ἰδών σε δαιτὶ κοινωνὸν καλεῖ .
sections.translation: Yes , and when he sees you there , he will invite you to the feast .

sections.label: Section 638
sections.original: πικρόν γε συνθοινάτορʼ , ἢν θεὸς θέλῃ .
sections.translation: I shall be a bitter companion in the feast , if the god wishes it .

sections.label: Section 639
sections.original: τοὐνθένδε πρὸς τὸ πῖπτον αὐτὸς ἐννόει .
sections.translation: After that , you yourself invent something , as it falls out .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας . — ἡ τεκοῦσα δʼ ἐστὶ ποῦ ;
sections.translation: Well said . But my mother , where is she ?

sections.label: Section 641
sections.original: Ἄργει · παρέσται δʼ οὖν πόσει θοίνην ἔπι .
sections.translation: At Argos ; but she will join her husband for the feast .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: τί δʼ οὐχ ἅμʼ ἐξωρμᾶτʼ ἐμὴ μήτηρ πόσει ;
sections.translation: Why didn ’ t my mother set out with her husband ?

sections.label: Section 643
sections.original: ψόγον τρέμουσα δημοτῶν ἐλείπετο .
sections.translation: From fear of the citizens ’ reproach she stayed behind .

sections.label: Section 644
sections.original: ξυνῆχʼ · ὕποπτος οὖσα γιγνώσκει πόλει .
sections.translation: I understand ; she knows that the city suspects her .

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: τοιαῦτα · μισεῖται γὰρ ἀνόσιος γυνή .
sections.translation: Something like that ; for an unholy woman is an object of hatred .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἐκείνην τόνδε τʼ ἐν ταὐτῷ κτενῶ ;
sections.translation: How then shall I kill her and him at once ?

sections.label: Section 647
sections.original: ἐγὼ φόνον γε μητρὸς ἐξαρτύσομαι .
sections.translation: I shall prepare my mother ’ s slaughter !

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐκεῖνά γʼ ἡ τύχη θήσει καλῶς .
sections.translation: And as for the other matter , fortune will ordain well .

sections.label: Section 649
sections.original: ὑπηρετείτω μὲν δυοῖν ὄντοιν ὅδε .
sections.translation: Let this man here help us with both .

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ · εὑρίσκεις δὲ μητρὶ πῶς φόνον ;
sections.translation: I will ; but how will you find a way to kill your mother ?

sections.label: Section 651
sections.original: λέγʼ , ὦ γεραιέ , τάδε Κλυταιμήστρᾳ μολών ·
sections.translation: Go to Clytemnestra , old man , and say this : report that I have given birth to a male child .

sections.label: Section 653
sections.original: πότερα πάλαι τεκοῦσαν ἢ νεωστὶ δή ;
sections.translation: That you have given birth some time ago , or quite recently ?

sections.label: Section 654
sections.original: δέχʼ ἡλίους , ἐν οἷσιν ἁγνεύει λεχώ .
sections.translation: Ten days ago , in which a woman who has given birth stays pure .

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: καὶ δὴ τί τοῦτο μητρὶ προσβάλλει φόνον ;
sections.translation: And how does this grant us the slaughter of your mother ?

sections.label: Section 656
sections.original: ἥξει κλύουσα λόχιά μου νοσήματα .
sections.translation: She will come , when she hears of my childbirth pangs .

sections.label: Section 657
sections.original: πόθεν ; τί δʼ αὐτῇ σοῦ μέλειν δοκεῖς , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: How is that ? Do you think she cares for you , child ?

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: ναί · καὶ δακρύσει γʼ ἀξίωμʼ ἐμῶν τόκων .
sections.translation: Yes ; and she will weep , surely , over my child ’ s low rank .

sections.label: Section 659
sections.original: ἴσως · πάλιν τοι μῦθον ἐς καμπὴν ἄγε .
sections.translation: Perhaps ; bring your story back again to the turning-point .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: ἐλθοῦσα μέντοι δῆλον ὡς ἀπόλλυται .
sections.translation: Well then , if she comes , it is clear that she will die .

sections.label: Section 661
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐπʼ αὐτάς γʼ εἶσι σῶν δόμων πύλας .
sections.translation: Yes , she will come right up to the door of your house .

sections.label: Section 662
sections.original: οὐκοῦν τραπέσθαι σμικρὸν εἰς Ἅιδου τόδε ;
sections.translation: Won ’ t it then be a little thing for her to turn aside to Hades ?

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ θάνοιμι τοῦτʼ ἰδὼν ἐγώ ποτε .
sections.translation: May I die , once I have seen this !

sections.label: Section 664
sections.original: πρώτιστα μέν νυν τῷδʼ ὑφήγησαι , γέρον —
sections.translation: First of all , old man , guide my brother —

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: Αἴγισθος ἔνθα νῦν θυηπολεῖ θεοῖς ;
sections.translation: To the place where Aegisthus is now sacrificing to the gods ?

sections.label: Section 666
sections.original: ἔπειτʼ ἀπαντῶν μητρὶ τἀπʼ ἐμοῦ φράσον .
sections.translation: Then , going to meet my mother , give her my message .

sections.label: Section 667
sections.original: ὥστʼ αὐτά γʼ ἐκ σοῦ στόματος εἰρῆσθαι δοκεῖν .
sections.translation: So that the very words will seem to have been said by you .

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: σὸν ἔργον ἤδη · πρόσθεν εἴληχας φόνου .
sections.translation: Your work begins at once ; you have drawn the first lot in the slaughter .
